title: Assay of urine Hg concentrations measurement
steps: All containers need to be soaked in nitric acid (10%) for 24h and then rinse with double-deionized water (DDW) .
Collect 50ml midstream urine with clean plastic container.
Add DDW to fill the volume up to 25 ml.

Switch on the HG - AFS 9700, and preheat 20 minutes. Urinary mercury is determined in the experimental conditions indicated in Table 1. ParametersHigh negative voltage of PMT270VAtomizer temperature Rome TemperatureAtomizer height 7 mmLamp current 30 mAFlow rate of carrier gas600 mL/minFlow rate of shield gas 800 mL/minMeasurement method Std. curveRead method Peak areaDelay time 3sRead time 24sInjection volume 2.0 mLResonance wavelength 253.7 nm Table1 The operating parameters of the AFS-9700 HG-AFS for the Determination of Hg Parameters High negative voltage of PMT270V Atomizer temperature Rome Temperature Atomizer height 7 mm Lamp current 30 mA Flow rate of carrier gas600 mL/min Flow rate of shield gas 800 mL/min Measurement method Std. curve Read method Peak area Delay time 3s Read time 24s Injection volume 2.0 mL Resonance wavelength 253.7 nm Table1 The operating parameters of the AFS-9700 HG-AFS for the Determination of Hg
Calibration regression curves at seven points (0, 0.2, 0.4, 0.8, 1.2, 1.6 and 2.0 ug/L) treated as the samples bromination procedure.
Calibration regression curves according to experimental conditions.
Spiked recovery test is performed to ensure the precision and accuracy of the analyses.
To avoid possible contamination during the digestion procedure and sample manipulation, a blank solution is used through the preparation and device assay process along every twenty samples.

A sample of standard materials with known reference concentrations of Hg is prepared and carried through the preparation and assay processes along every twenty urine samples to verify the stability of the process.

Transfer 2 ml of the urine sample to a into 25 ml caped glass tubes. Add 5 mL of 8mol/L nitric acid, anti-foaming agent (a few drops) and 2 ml of 0.0599mol/L potassium bromate - 0.0234mol/L potassium bromide reagent, respectively
Tightly capped glass tubes and allow standing on bench for 20 min to complete breakdown of organic Hg compounds.
Add 10ml DDW, then add a few drops of 1.439mol/L hydroxylamine hydrochloride to remove free bromine after digestion.
creator: Lu Zhao Xu
